meta.footEn: Data snapshot: 2026-07-09 · Garmin fenix 8 via Freddy

en.c5d: computed from sleep, HRV, RHR, stress & recent load
en.rec: A shorter, fair night to cap the week: 6h31 at score 77 off a 22:27 bedtime — 27 minutes under the 7-day average (6h58) and a clear step down from the recent run of 84–91s. Deep sleep was actually the week's best at 1h57, but REM was compressed to 1h19 and an early 04:58 wake cut the night short. The autonomic side is the story this morning: HRV fell to 32 (−6 from the previous night and 8 below the 7-day baseline of 40) and RHR climbed to 58 (+3 vs yesterday, the highest reading of the week) — the cumulative cost of a full training week. Morning stress is still calm at 26. Nights are labeled by the evening they start, so this one sits under Wed 8 (Wednesday–Thursday); Thursday's night (Jul 2) remains the untracked slot on the chart. Readiness is moderate — the dip is expected after the week's load, not a red flag, but with a squat session planned today, treat the warm-up as the gauge and don't force the top set if the bar feels heavy.

hr.c5d: izračunato iz sna, HRV-a, RHR-a, stresa i opterećenja
hr.rec: Kraća, osrednja noć za kraj tjedna: 6h31 uz ocjenu 77 i lijeganje u 22:27 — 27 minuta ispod 7-dnevnog prosjeka (6h58) i jasan korak niže od nedavnog niza 84–91. Duboki san bio je zapravo najbolji u tjednu, 1h57, ali REM se stisnuo na 1h19, a rano buđenje u 04:58 skratilo je noć. Autonomna slika je glavna priča jutros: HRV je pao na 32 (−6 u odnosu na prethodnu noć i 8 ispod 7-dnevne osnovice od 40), a RHR se popeo na 58 (+3 vs jučer, najviše očitanje u tjednu) — kumulativna cijena punog tjedna treninga. Jutarnji stres i dalje miran, 26. Noći se bilježe prema večeri kojom počinju, pa ova stoji pod Sri 8 (srijeda–četvrtak); četvrtkova noć (2.7.) i dalje je nezabilježeni termin na grafu. Spremnost je umjerena — pad je očekivan nakon tjednog opterećenja, ne alarm, ali uz planirani trening čučnja danas, neka zagrijavanje bude mjerilo i ne forsiraj radnu seriju ako šipka djeluje teško.
